evidence: In December 2024, Visa acquired Featurespace Limited, a developer of real-time artificial intelligence payments protection technology...for a purchase consideration of $946 million.

Visa s commitment to innovation and collaboration is reflected in how we equip employees with GenAI capabilities...our internal GenAI hub that serves as an easy-to-access entry point to AI models, applications and developer tools.
VCA is comprised of specialized advisory practices such as strategy and commercial money movement, portfolio optimization, digital, AI, stablecoin, cybersecurity, risk and implementation support.

From its launch in March 2025 through the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, it has been embraced by nearly 26,000 employees launching over 261,000 AI-powered chats to get answers, automate tasks and accelerate outcomes.
Visa Consumer Authentication Service provides an AI-powered, real-time, network-agnostic risk score that enables clients to reduce fraud and false declines for intelligent authentication decisions.
In 2025, we deployed a pilot program that processed live agentic token transactions within the ecommerce and enterprise environments.

The adoption of agentic commerce...presents novel and complex regulatory, privacy and cybersecurity risks...we may see increased instances of erroneous or disputed payments, increased chargebacks and reputational harm.
Reliance on agentic AI introduces challenges in monitoring cross-border, prohibited or high-risk transactions, where conflicting regulatory requirements may apply.
The EU has adopted a comprehensive AI Act...with key provisions for high-risk AI systems taking effect in August 2026. Meanwhile, several U.S. states...have adopted AI-specific frameworks.

capex_mentions.quote: acquired Featurespace Limited...for a purchase consideration of $946 million
capex_mentions.context: Fiscal 2025 acquisition of AI payments protection technology company, December 2024

limitations: No multi-year AI capex program or quantified ongoing AI infrastructure spend disclosed beyond the Featurespace acquisition; investment intensity capped at 4 rather than 5.

Risk disclosure is detailed and company-specific with named mechanisms (chargebacks, cross-border monitoring, regulatory fragmentation) but no quantified risk exposure or named internal AI governance body, keeping score at 4 rather than 5.
Promotional language present in some AI narrative sections (e.g., 'innovative offering', 'empowering partners'); scored on deployed use cases with quantified metrics where available.
